binary.text: Closing the Gender Gap in Agriculture: Experimental Evidence from Uganda — It is a well-established fact that plots cultivated by female farmers in Sub-Saharan Africa are less productive than those managed by male farmers, but the reasons behind this gap are unclear. In this project, we collaborate with the NGO BRAC in Uganda to evaluate the impact of an intervention that aims to empower female farmers by improving their soft skills and their aspirations. In addition to studying how these interventions change female farmers' productivity, we evaluate the effects of providing information about soil testing services and study how the empowerment program affects the take up of these techniques by female relative to male farmers. The project will shed light on the extent to which lack of soft skills and aspirations may be driving the gender gap in technology adoption and agricultural product

Integrated and innovative maritime technologies for mobility and fisheries — The Marshall Islands are a small low-lying atoll nation in the Pacific Ocean. Its very existence depends on ambitious global emission reduction to limit sea level rise. Despite this, it heavily relies on costly imported fossil fuels for its vital sea transport and fishing sector. The country works towards developing a zero-emission fleet as part of ongoing initiatives, but a successful paradigm shift throughout society is still pending. The project merges emission-free boat design and construction, indigenous knowledge and modern maritime training for small-scale transport and fisheries. This breaks social and gender barriers, combining efforts in society and the private sector. Integrating innovative technologies into formal education fosters societal sustainability and resilience. binary.text: Strengthening the Kenyan oilseed value chain: helping farmers with new products, new processing capacity and new markets — The global growth in specialty oil seed exports and consumption has been 10% per annum for the last 10 years. Specialty oils are increasingly used in food, cosmetic and pharmaceutical applications. Despite the global growth, Kenyan oil seed farmers and producers have not benefited from this growth potential. Among specialty oil seeds grown in Kenya are macadamia, cashew, sesame, baobab, avocado, shea, passion fruit and neem. Often the seeds are discarded or sold cheaply on the local market. The purpose of this project is to strengthen the Kenyan oilseed value chain. It will bring new Kenyan oilseed products to the market through enhanced and more efficient growing, collecting and processing capacity, thus opening new markets for Kenyan oilseed products. Sustainable Winter Tourism Kyrgyzstan, Phase II — This project supports Kyrgyzstan in realizing the great potential of its tourism sector. Tourism in Kyrgyzstan can become an important contributor to economic growth, creating income and economic opportunities for a wide range of people. To realize its full potential, the sector needs to overcome competitiveness constraints, such as low productivity and insufficient capacity for sector governance, and tackle overarching sustainability issues which are likely to increase as more tourist arrive. A two-year pilot phase from 2019-2021 confirmed the viability of developing winter tourism, allowed to build relationships with key partners in the sector, and served to identify suitable entry points.

PIN Kenya helps internally displaced families and members of the Ogiek tribe, who lost their homes and territory over the years – they have spent years in slums and today have decided to make a living again in the fields where they have been relocated by the government. The basic engines of these communities are women. In the Nakuru region, in almost every village, one can find women 's groups working together to develop their communities – they take care of family finances, childcare, gardens and animals. binary.text: Act for the Environment! Keep it clean cooperation program in Georgia — The aim of the project is to prevent illegal dumping in the Khashur and Kaspi regions of Georgia, in cooperation between communities and local governments. As a result of the project, the awareness about the impact of waste on nature has increased significantly, a new social norm has been created that illegal waste dumping is not acceptable. The waste bins and cigarette butt boxes have been placed in the so-called waste hot spots of the regions and they will be kept clean and waste-free also after finishing the project. Technology will be used for mapping waste.The end result of the project a successful, sustainable form of cooperation to address the problem of illegal dumping and prevent uncontrolled littering has created, which can be implemented by other regions of Georgia.
binary.tech_reason: The project uses technology for waste mapping and fosters community-government cooperation, aligning with digital governance initiatives.

We have identified a severe disease, caused by a group of four Botryosphaeria fungi, as a main factor limiting the sustainable cultivation of the popular tree species. The fungi infect, in concert, and cause very severe and widespread disease on Grevillea robusta, Eucalyptus spp., Senna siamea and Azadiracta indica, all exotics in the region. The indigenous and drought resistant species, Melia volkensii seems resistant to the disease. The disease is most prevalent and severe in drylands. multiway.text: Strengthening Kenya¿s Security and Cybersecurity — Security threats and challenges have considerable impacted Kenya¿s ability to achieve its development goals. Conflict and instability in the region, in addition to internal political and security challenges can fuel unrest, in particular when combined with tribalism, corruption, impunity and unresolved historical grievances. In addition, Kenya¿s ongoing digital transformation comes with increased risks posed by insufficient cybersecurity safeguards. This action will seek to work to reinforce two interconnected strands of security: (1) Preventing and countering violent extremism (PCVE)  (2) Strengthening the resilience of the cybersecurity ecosystem. By enabling a more secure environment, including in the digital space, the Action directly contributes to the EU-Kenya Green and Digital Partnership under the Global Gateway
multiway.purpose: Civilian peace-building, conflict prevention and resolution
multiway.tech_reason: The project focuses on enhancing Kenya's cybersecurity ecosystem and policy frameworks, aligning with digital governance objectives.

nontech.text: Individualized physical activity treatment for drug naive type 2 diabetics in Vietnam — The proportion of diabetes in Vietnam is rapidly growing, similarly to what is happening in most middle-income countries in the world. This increase is largely related to rapid changes in life style including a more unhealthy diet and a marked reduction of physical activity (PA). In the wake of the increased prevalence of diabetes, cardiovascular morbidity and mortality rises. This puts a large toll on GDP growth and the economical situation for families, of which many risk poverty. Diabetes can, cost-effectively, both be prevented and treated with improved life style, e.g. increased PA. The main purpose of this study is to compare clinical and quality of life outcomes of an Individualized PA (IPA) intervention programme with the outcomes of standard care (SC) for newly diagnosed type 2 diabetes (T2D)
nontech.tech_reason: The project uses digital tools (e.g., weekly telephone calls) to deliver individualized physical activity education and support, targeting underserved diabetic patients to improve health outcomes and inclusion.

nontech.text: Promoting cultivation of disease resistant native tree species as alternatives to the Botryospharia-disease susceptible Grevillea robusta in hot and dry areas of Kenya and Uganda. — Tree health is of increasing importance in forestry and agroforestry in East Africa. We have identified a severe disease, caused by a group of four Botryosphaeria fungi, as a main factor limiting the sustainable cultivation of the popular tree species. The fungi infect, in concert, and cause very severe and widespread disease on Grevillea robusta, Eucalyptus spp., Senna siamea and Azadiracta indica, all exotics in the region. The indigenous and drought resistant species, Melia volkensii seems resistant to the disease. The disease is most prevalent and severe in drylands. nontech.text: Secondary school, quality education and practical skills gains for young people in the rural area on Rusinga Island. — In line with the Slovak Republic Strategy for Development Cooperation with the Republic of Kenya for the period 2014-2018, the project promotes sustainable and equitable socio-economic development of the Republic of Kenya in education and agriculture. Development problem - the isolated Rusinga Island in Lake Victoria in western Kenya has about 22,000 inhabitants, of whom about half are children under 15 years. As a result of HIV/AIDS, malnutrition, poor hygiene and morbidity, the island has one of the highest mortality rates, both in Kenya and in the world, and therefore a large numbers of orphans. Our organization's project on the island began in 2005, we have been slowly improving the health status of the local community by the successful operation of the health center

nontech.text: Engaging Stakeholders in Environmental Conservation Phase II (ESEC II) — Artisanal and small-scale mining (ASM) is a poverty-driven phenomena that contributes importantly to rural job creation, income generation and poverty reduction. nontech.text: Strengthening the Kenyan oilseed value chain: helping farmers with new products, new processing capacity and new markets — The global growth in specialty oil seed exports and consumption has been 10% per annum for the last 10 years. The purpose of this project is to strengthen the Kenyan oilseed value chain. nontech.text: Swisspeace: Dealing with the Past Course 2015 — Dealing with the Past (DwP) is a priority for the Swiss Government. This course is offered in order to enhance the understanding and capacities for a comprehensive and inclusive approach to DwP and to promote a holistic understanding of DwP strategies within the Swiss Government and among bilateral and multilateral partners and Swiss and non-Swiss professionals work-ing in the field of peace building. nontech.text: Engaging Stakeholders in Environmental Conservation Phase II (ESEC II) — Artisanal and small-scale mining (ASM) is a poverty-driven phenomena that contributes importantly to rural job creation, income generation and poverty reduction. There is thus an urgent need to determine molecular mechanisms underlying this increased risk and propose therapeutic interventions. The same progesterone-related effect is also seen during the luteal phase of the menstrual cycle, a phase also associated with higher infection risk. nontech.text: F.a: Busia Town Council Area Development Project — The programme aims to improve the quality of life of community members, especially vulnerable children, in Busia town, East Uganda. The programme focuses on vulnerable population, such as youth, underaged mothers, street children and children involved in illegal trade. Parents, teachers, local politicians and other stakeholders are trained on child rights issues. Families livelihoods opportunities are being strengthened in order to ensure children's opportunities for education and to prevent children from child labor. Youth are trained for e.g. hairdressing, tailoring and bakery professions. Quality health services are promoted, especially to ensure services for pregnant women and children. nontech.text: Improving sustainability of molecular genetic laboratory with implementation of modern molecular genetic tests for patients with malignant diseases — As part of the project, the molecular genetics laboratory was fully equipped and successfully performs all the necessary tests of state-of-the-art technology needed for the diagnosis of onco-hematological diseases in children, and is one of the advanced diagnostic laboratories for oncological and hematological diseases in Georgia. nontech.text: Tourist InfoCenter in Nivica — The aim of the presented project was to help create conditions in the village for the development of tourism and agrotourism, and thus attract tourists to the hitherto not very well known, but attractive locality, which will contribute to the development of the village, employment and entrepreneurship of local residents in related services. The subject of the project was the reconstruction of a dilapidated building on the main square of the village Nivica into a tourist information center and its equipment with furniture and information technology, as well as tourist brochures and an outdoor information panel with a map of tourist routes in the area. Czech volunteers are already working in the village, working with the Albanian National Coastal Agency to mark hiking trails. Czech archaeologists also work here.
nontech.tech_reason: The project provides digital access and information services to tourists and locals, enhancing digital inclusion through a community information center.

nontech.text: Humanitarian Law Center - Expanding the reach and impact of the Documentation Center - Kosovo — Opposing narratives and various interpretations on the Kosovo conflict are still very present in general public. With a set of activities promoting the judicially established facts, this project will contribute to the process of reconciliation by reaching a considerate number of people in Kosovo and offer a common remembrance on the conflict.
nontech.tech_reason: The project expands access to documented historical facts through multimedia exhibitions and educational activities, targeting underserved communities to foster shared understanding and reconciliation.

nontech.text: Soutien humanitaire au secteur scolaire dans les zones de conflit de l'Ukraine orientale pour promouvoir le développement durable dans l'est du pays — Leur organisation était à l'origine spécialisée dans la lutte contre les mines terrestres. L'Ukraine a subi de nombreux dommages de guerre. Les infrastructures scolaires ont été détruites. Vous avez noté les effets dévastateurs du conflit en cours sur l'accès des enfants à l'éducation. Avec ce projet, vous supprimez les obstacles physiques à la scolarisation (réhabilitation des installations scolaires, des salles de classe, des cantines et des toilettes). Ils rétablissent un transport fiable pour les écoliers. Grâce à un soutien financier, vous fournissez aux enfants du matériel pédagogique et un accès à Internet. Vous impliquez également les aspects sociaux du projet, tels que le travail de sensibilisation et les discussions.
nontech.tech_reason: The project provides internet access and educational materials to children in conflict zones, directly addressing digital access barriers for underserved groups.

nontech.text: Prêt à Guaranty Trust Bank : fournir des financements à long terme aux entreprises nigérianes — Proparco soutient l'activité de crédit à moyen et long termes de la Guaranty Trust Bank, au Nigeria. La banque finance les entreprises nigérianes – dans un contexte où les ressources longues en devises étrangères restent rares. Fondée comme banque d'affaires en 1990, Guaranty Trust Bank (GTBank) a obtenu une licence de banque universelle en 2002. Elle est présente au Nigéria à travers un réseau de 240 agences. La banque mise de plus en plus sur l'e-banking avec 48 e-agences. Le groupe GTB développe son réseau à l'international : il compte une filiale en Gambie, en Sierra Leone, au Ghana, au Liberia, au Royaume Uni, en Côte d'Ivoire et au Kenya. La ligne de crédit de Proparco est destinée au financement de l'activité de crédit à moyen et long terme de GTBank auprès des entreprises. Cette opérat
nontech.tech_reason: The project supports financial inclusion by expanding access to credit and digital banking services for Nigerian businesses and underserved groups.

nontech.text: F.a: Busia Town Council Area Development Project — The programme aims to improve the quality of life of community members, especially vulnerable children, in Busia town, East Uganda. In this border town, children's safety is hampered by illegal trade, poor sanitation facilities and child neglect and abuse. The programme focuses on vulnerable population, such as youth, underaged mothers, street children and children involved in illegal trade. Parents, teachers, local politicians and other stakeholders are trained on child rights issues. Families livelihoods opportunities are being strengthened in order to ensure children's opportunities for education and to prevent children from child labor. Youth are trained for e.g. hairdressing, tailoring and bakery professions. Quality health services are promoted, especially to ensure services for pregnant women and children. Through community action

nontech.text: Safe and respectful maternity care: Setting up a network for research in tertiary hospitals — At least two million lives are lost every year globally due to complications in the intrapartum period. We aim to define and innovate interventions and perform implementation research focused on the recently published new intrapartum care guidelines of the World Health Organisations. Respectful intrapartum care shall be in the centre, and we will assess the potential of lactate point-of-care testing to diagnose hypoxia and fetal monitoring using the new Moyo Fetal Heart Rate Monitor.Network partners are Makerere University and Iganga hospital, Uganda, Muhimbili University Hospital and Aga Khan University, Tanzania, t

nontech.text: DMDP 2020 - ICCO, Filippinerne — 'More with Less in the Philippine Coconut Industry'The 'More with Less in the Philippine Coconut Industry' project by ICCO, Lionheart and PCA will contribute to SDG8 by stimulating higher levels of economic activity in the coconut sector through technological upgrading and innovation, and by focusing on safe and labor intensive production of high-value added products from coconut sap. By introducing planting materials of superior variety, coupled with environmental friendly Good Agricultural Practices, yield will increase. Introducing innovative technologies in sap tapping will make the task less difficult, thereby increasing productivity and generating jobs. nontech.tech_reason: The project enhances farmer productivity and income through technological upgrades and training, directly targeting underserved agricultural communities to improve livelihoods and economic inclusion.

nontech.text: Humanitarian Law Center - Expanding the reach and impact of the Documentation Center - Kosovo — Opposing narratives and various interpretations on the Kosovo conflict are still very present in general public. Humanitarian Law Center established the Documentation Center with a permanent multimedia exhibition on judicially established facts by International Criminal Tribunal for the Former Yugoslavia (ICTY). Five judicial proceedings presented in 9 videos are offering an inclusive and victims oriented narrative. With a set of activities promoting the judicially established facts, this project will contribute to the process of reconciliation by reaching a considerate number of people in Kosovo and offer a common remembrance on the conflict.

The Hospital has a capacity of 150 beds. It provides healthcare and treatment for sick people and promotes health education and prevention with special attention to the problem of AIDS.
nontech.tech_reason: The project involves procurement of physical medical and operational equipment (e.g., X-ray viewers, suction machines, UPS) to establish functional health departments, aligning with hard infrastructure development.

nontech.text: Expansion of the structure of St. John's High School in Pathein — The project envisages the extension of the existing school by adding two more floors to the current building with classrooms, rooms for teachers and computer rooms. The aim is to increase the number of pupils attending school, which can now only accommodate 150/200 children.
nontech.tech_reason: The addition of computer rooms aims to improve digital access and skills for underserved students, supporting educational inclusion.

nontech.text: Reconstruction of School Facilities in Timor Leste — Thanks to its commitment for socially deprived people and a peaceful cohabitation after the independence from Indonesia, the Instituto de Ciencias Religiosas is a highly acknowledged institution in Timor Leste. The main objective of the project is the improvement of the learning environment of the Institute through the reconstruction of the conference hall and the IT room. Both facilities cannot be used at the moment due to its bad conditions. By improving this situation, the institute aims to further improve the education and quality of its students.
nontech.tech_reason: The project upgrades an IT room and conference hall to enhance digital learning access for underserved students, directly supporting digital inclusion.

nontech.text: Wells and toilet facilities for the Brain Hill Primary and Secondary School — The aim of this project is to equip the Brain Hill Primary School with a well that will be used by both the 410 students and the entire village community. So far there is no safe source of drinking water in Nakatete. People feed themselves on a body of water that is sometimes also used by wild animals. Furthermore, the previously precarious hygienic conditions at the school are to be improved by building a toilet facility. As part of this project, Wiwo e. V. works with the Ugandan NGO Rural Health Care Foundation (RHCF). RHCF runs various projects in the field of health, education and hygiene and has developed into an expert in water supply and toilet construction in very remote schools.
nontech.purpose: Basic drinking water supply and basic sanitation
nontech.tech_reason: The project improves access to essential services and community connectivity through water and sanitation infrastructure, indirectly supporting digital inclusion by enabling broader community development.

nontech.text: Energy Efficient Urban District — Ukrainian residential buildings still have a lot of potential where energy efficiency is concerned – and this applies to both old and new buildings, with the result that the construction sector today accounts for around 40% of energy consumption in the country. The project showed how energy can be saved and climate-damaging emissions reduced using a model construction complex in Kiev. Equipped with modern technologies like high-insulation windows, heat pumps and a ventilation system, the renovated part of the complex serves as a showcase for the planning, construction and operation of an energy-efficient and environmentally friendly building. The project shared its experience with the Ukrainian construction sector and universities with the aim of integrating energy efficiency more intensively into the training of construction specialists. nontech.tech_reason: The project improves regulatory and institutional frameworks for energy‑efficient construction, a digital governance function.

By introducing planting materials of superior variety, coupled with environmental friendly Good Agricultural Practices, yield will increase. nontech.text: Providing better infrastructure and access to social services for people with disabilities in Georgia — Projekt mial na celu uruchomienie 3 centrów opieki dziennej dla dzieci i doroslych z niepelnosprawnoscia w Gruzji. Zostal on skoordynowany i opracowany w konsultacji z 3 krajowymi NGO: Society Biliki, Child & Environment, Child, Family, Society (CFS) oraz powiazanymi departamentami samorzadu lokalnego. Panstwowa agencja pomocy spolecznej w Gruzji i Agencja Opieki Spolecznej (SSA) zapewnila budynki (domy) wydzierzawione odpowiednim krajowym NGO. Wymagaly one jednak calkowitej renowacji. Prace remontowe zostaly zlecone uslugodawcy budowlanemu, po przejsciu procedury przetargowej. Fundusze zostaly udostepnione wykonawcy w 3 ratach po osiagnieciu uzgodnionego procentu pracy i pozyskaniu materialów. Równolegle z dzialaniami zwiazanymi z remontem realizowane byly inne dzialania: przeprowadzo
nontech.tech_reason: The project includes training sessions on independent living skills and behavior management for staff, targeting underserved groups (people with disabilities) to improve access to social services through digital literacy and capacity building.

Poprawa infrastruktury: stworzenie 20 modelowych sal przedszkolnych (M I), budowa 10 innowacyjnych placów zabaw, wspierajacych rozwój motoryki i sensoryki dzieci (M II), ocena stanu infrastruktury 80 przedszkoli, poprawa dostepnosci 20 przedszkoli - zbudowanie podjazdów i oznakowanie (M III). 2. Szkolenia nauczycielek i metodyczek w zakresie innowacyjnych podejsc w pracy z dziecmi, multiplikacja wiedzy wsród nauczycielek przedszkoli w rej
nontech.tech_reason: The project emphasizes capacity building, training for teachers and local officials, parental engagement, and the creation of an informational portal to improve access and quality of preschool education for underserved communities.

nontech.text: The increase of awareness about pollution in Tumanyan region — The project builds on the good experience of using hard data to improve the situation of communities affected by industrial pollution in Tumanyan region. In the project we will conduct pollution monitoring in new localities and launch an interactive map using the results of previous measurements. Several active representatives of local civil society will be trained to provide basic consultations to local communities and their integration into international civil society networks addressing similar issues will be strengthened. Also their skill for negotiating with municipalities will be improved. In response to the already identified needs of the local population,monitoring of the health of the population related to pollution problems and analysis of health care will be carried out. The project also includes activities aimed a
nontech.tech_reason: The project trains local civil society representatives and improves their skills for community engagement and advocacy, directly targeting underserved groups and building digital capacity for inclusion.

nontech.text: Act for the Environment! Keep it clean cooperation program in Georgia — The aim of the project is to prevent illegal dumping in the Khashur and Kaspi regions of Georgia, in cooperation between communities and local governments. As a result of the project, the awareness about the impact of waste on nature has increased significantly, a new social norm has been created that illegal waste dumping is not acceptable. The waste bins and cigarette butt boxes have been placed in the so-called waste hot spots of the regions and they will be kept clean and waste-free also after finishing the project. Technology will be used for mapping waste.The end result of the project a successful, sustainable form of cooperation to address the problem of illegal dumping and prevent uncontrolled littering has created, which can be implemented by other regions of Georgia.
nontech.tech_reason: The project uses technology for waste mapping and fosters community-government cooperation, aligning with digital governance initiatives.

nontech.text: PhD student scholarship in Agrobiodiversity — The PhD Programme in Emerging Digital Technologies is a three-year programme which provides an exhaustive training path with structured teaching and supervised research activities.  In line with the standards of excellence pursued by the School, the objective of the programme is to train a professional figure able to successfully integrate in both national and international, public and private research facilities and in companies manufacturing products or service providers, operating in the field of communication, information and perception technologies.
nontech.tech_reason: The scholarship supports advanced research training in digital technologies, aiming to develop skilled professionals for inclusive innovation and broader societal impact.

nontech.text: Multidisciplinary technologies for the study and conservation of Post Byzantine monasteries in Southern Albania — The project, active since 2019, investigates the post-Byzantine monasteries of southern Albania, which constitute heritage of extraordinary value, currently under threat. The project seeks to support the study and safeguarding of three religious complexes, applying an integrated program that starts from historical-architectural research and develops with innovative 3D scanning and modelling technologies, georadar diagnostics, semantic HBIM platform for the conservation project. Courses are foreseen for local officials and experts.
nontech.tech_reason: The project involves digital documentation and modeling technologies for heritage conservation, which aligns with digital governance through the use of advanced ICT tools for public sector applications.

To increase technical and personal capacities on drought management and public awareness about drought impacts on the water resources through training, workshops and dialogues on mitigation measures. The outcomes will be: 1.1. Proposal of the Drought Action Plan for Drin River Basin as integrated part of the River Basin Management Plan as required by the EU Water Framework Directive. 1.2. Port
nontech.tech_reason: The project develops a national drought management platform and governance mechanisms, aligning with digital public service and policy frameworks.

nontech.text: The main objective of the CLIMATHON ALBANIA project is to improve the quality of life and health of the people of Albania by introducing innovations to improve sustainability and environmental protection. The project is focused on the transfer of practical experience from the successful implementation of innovation events CLIMATHON BRATISLAVA. The event involves startups, technology companies, student teams, and experienced mentors whose goal is to create prototypes of innovative solutions in 48 hours that respond to the challenges the cities are facing. CLIMATHON ALBANIA will be attended by more than 170 participants who will develop at least 15 innovative solutions. The project has two specific objectives, (1) to raise awareness of green innovation and capacity building in cities to implement green and innovative solutions, and (2) to implement innovative solutions that will contribute
nontech.tech_reason: The project emphasizes capacity building, gender inclusion, and empowering diverse participants (including women and youth) to develop climate solutions, aligning with digital inclusion goals.

nontech.text: Advisor for maintenance and repair of facilities of Mukuru Promotion Center, Nairobi — The organisation started as a temporary school in the Mukuru slums and its key objective was to provide education to the children of Mukuru to get them off the streets. Mukuru Promotion Centre runs four primary schools, a secondary school and an  adult education centre. On vocational training, the MPC runs a vocational centre offering courses in computer, beauty and hairdressing, dress making, plumbing and masonry, catering as well as art. Since 1st March 2016, a technical advisor was  assigned to support selected MPC staff in repair and maintenance works for MPC schools and institutions. When the workload is heavier, selected jobs are given to local tradesmen who carry it out under the supervision of the TA.
nontech.tech_reason: The project supports vocational training and capacity building for underserved communities, aligning with digital inclusion goals.

nontech.text: Advancing knowledge on the epidemiology of African swine fever virus in Kenya — In East Africa, pig production is linked to household livelihoods, as a source of protein and income while significantly contributing to the national economy. However, pig production has never really picked up due to repeated outbreaks of African swine fever (ASF) causing nearly 100% mortality of affected pigs. The spread of ASF out of Africa is a significant threat to global food security. The epidemiology of ASF virus in the Kenyan wild reservoirs is insufficiently understood. Specifically, do warthogs and ticks carry more diverse ASFV genotypes than domestic pigs? What is the prevalence of the virus in warthog populations across ecological zones? We focus on these questions as warthogs and their soft ticks are territorial and have a long lifespan, and may serve as sentinels for understanding the virus's ep
nontech.tech_reason: The project builds research capacity and disseminates findings, aligning with digital inclusion goals of skill development and knowledge sharing.

nontech.text: WaterNet:Safe drinking water in Ukraine — NGO WaterNet propose the project 'Safe drinking water in Ukraine: Quality assessment and methods of control' for 20 months duration aims to raise awareness about the drinking water quality in Ukraine during the war and sharing the knowledges about modern water treatment methods and technologies. It is expected: new open-access Water Quality Map with results of 2000 water samples analyses, practical recommendations on drinking water quality improvement both for individual households and for communities, online training courses on modern water treatment methods and technologies for academic units, students and specialists from water treatment industry and small public water utilities and public information dissemination.
nontech.purpose: Water sector policy and administrative management
nontech.tech_reason: The project delivers open-access water quality data, training courses, and practical recommendations to improve digital access to knowledge and skills for underserved communities and professionals in Ukraine.

nontech.text: F.a: Building Strong Unions and Strengthening Workers' Rights in Indonesia — The project will organise and build capacities of Indonesian metal and mining union leaders and members, with a special attention on women and youth to institutionalise their participation in unions' leadership and activities. Capacity building will focus on the ability to campaign for union representation and occupational healthcare system committees at workplaces, ratification of the ILO conventions 176, 183 and 190, and for amendment of the so-called Omnibus law in Indonesia. Organising, campaigning and collective negotiations will explore the use of technology to facilitate and improve unions' work. The project will also level-up the dialogue between educational / training institutions and trade unions to integrate trade union education in the academic and vocational curriculum, particularly for the graduati
nontech.purpose: Ending violence against women and girls
nontech.tech_reason: The project uses technology to empower unions and improve workers' rights, focusing on capacity building and inclusion of women and youth, aligning with digital inclusion objectives.

nontech.text: Permaculture Action for Sustainable Savings And Greening Entrepreneurship for school children (PASSAGE) — The PASSAGE project aims to promote financial self-reliance of vulnerable school children and their households in Amuru district, Acholi, Uganda.10 state schools are involved in the educational project. In addition, permaculture clubs will be formed in 20 villages in the region. Participants of school and village clubs are trained in entrepreneurship education as well as to participate in dialogue in their communities and e.g. radio talk shows to promote human rights. Those involved in the project are also trained and supported in economics (saving), adopting good nutrition and protecting the environment.About 200,000 people live in Amuru district. More than half of them are under 20 years of age. The area is very poor. The Acholi region has the lowest proportion of food-poor househo
nontech.tech_reason: The project builds financial literacy, entrepreneurship, and digital rights awareness among school children and rural communities, directly targeting underserved groups to improve digital and economic inclusion.

Participants of school and village clubs are trained in entrepreneurship education as well as to participate in dialogue in their communities and e.g. radio talk shows to promote human rights. Those involved in the project are also trained and supported in economics (saving), adopting good nutrition and protecting the environment.About 200,000 people live in Amuru district. More than half of them are under 20 years of age. The area is very poor. The Acholi region has the lowest proportion of food-poor househo
nontech.tech_reason: The project trains school children, mothers, and community members in financial literacy, entrepreneurship, and digital communication (e.g., radio talk shows), directly enhancing digital skills and inclusion for vulnerable populations.

nontech.text: F.a: Kenya COVID-19 2020 — Kenya Red Cross aims at preventing spreading of COVID-19 by training volunteers and staff in epidemic control and mobilizing them. Crisis awareness is promoted in communities and various communication campaigns will be organized. Red Cross also supports hygiene and handwashing practices and facilities. Red Cross supports health authorities e.g. through screening, tracking and referrals.
nontech.tech_reason: The project trains volunteers and staff, promotes community awareness, and supports hygiene practices, focusing on capacity building and outreach rather than physical or governance infrastructure.

nontech.text: NORHED II Water ESSENCE Africa — The Water ESSENCE Africa - creating synergy to meet the global challenges project addresses the challanges related to unevenly distributed freshwater. The proejct addresses these challanges through a matrix of three vertical cross-disciplinary issues on health, climate change, and energy, and two horizontal topics: gender equality and digitalisation of education.
nontech.tech_reason: The project emphasizes digitalisation of education and gender equality, targeting underserved groups through inclusive digital capacity building.

nontech.text: Humanitarian help to Ukraine, coordination of the goods donated by Latvian enetrprises — Project is aimed on ensure logistics (planning and coordination of company goods, ensurance of warehouse work, marking and accounting of goods, monitoring the technical loading of goods, communication with service providers and cargo planning), within humanitarian help to Ukraine, including coordination of project team work, preparation of meetings and reports, provision of IT work, cargo planning, communication with recipients, as well as documentation and financial management (preparation and coordination of bills, preparation of cargo documentation, accounting and financial planning, provision of accounting work).
nontech.tech_reason: The project involves coordination, reporting, financial management, and logistics planning using IT systems, aligning with digital governance functions like e-government coordination and digital public service management.

nontech.text: Initative TSARA : recherche en collaboration dans le domaine agronomique — L'initiative TSARA a pour objectif de produire un programme de recherche et d'innovation co- construit entre institutions africaines et françaises, qui soit à la fois de haute qualité et tourné vers l'impact, la formation, le transfert auprès des acteurs du monde rural et urbain et l'appui aux politiques publiques.  Elle s'appuie sur une coordination étroite entre INRAE, le Cirad et un ensemble d'institutions africaines ayant participé à diverses réunions virtuelles en 2021 et 2022.
nontech.tech_reason: The project emphasizes collaborative research, capacity building, and knowledge transfer to rural and urban stakeholders, aligning with digital inclusion goals.

nontech.text: Act for the Environment! Keep it clean cooperation program in Georgia — The aim of the project is to prevent illegal dumping in the Khashur and Kaspi regions of Georgia, in cooperation between communities and local governments. As a result of the project, the awareness about the impact of waste on nature has increased significantly, a new social norm has been created that illegal waste dumping is not acceptable. The waste bins and cigarette butt boxes have been placed in the so-called waste hot spots of the regions and they will be kept clean and waste-free also after finishing the project. Technology will be used for mapping waste.The end result of the project a successful, sustainable form of cooperation to address the problem of illegal dumping and prevent uncontrolled littering has created, which can be implemented by other regions of Georgia.
nontech.tech_reason: The project uses digital mapping technology to coordinate waste management and foster sustainable cooperation between communities and local governments, aligning with e‑government and digital public service objectives.

nontech.text: Act for the Environment! Keep it clean cooperation program in Georgia — The aim of the project is to prevent illegal dumping in the Khashur and Kaspi regions of Georgia, in cooperation between communities and local governments. As a result of the project, the awareness about the impact of waste on nature has increased significantly, a new social norm has been created that illegal waste dumping is not acceptable. The waste bins and cigarette butt boxes have been placed in the so-called waste hot spots of the regions and they will be kept clean and waste-free also after finishing the project. Technology will be used for mapping waste.The end result of the project a successful, sustainable form of cooperation to address the problem of illegal dumping and prevent uncontrolled littering has created, which can be implemented by other regions of Georgia.
nontech.tech_reason: The project uses technology for waste mapping and fosters community-government cooperation, aligning with digital governance initiatives.

nontech.text: DMDP 2020 - ICCO, Filippinerne — 'More with Less in the Philippine Coconut Industry'The 'More with Less in the Philippine Coconut Industry' project by ICCO, Lionheart and PCA will contribute to SDG8 by stimulating higher levels of economic activity in the coconut sector through technological upgrading and innovation, and by focusing on safe and labor intensive production of high-value added products from coconut sap. By introducing planting materials of superior variety, coupled with environmental friendly Good Agricultural Practices, yield will increase. Introducing innovative technologies in sap tapping will make the task less difficult, thereby increasing productivity and generating jobs. These twin interventions result in increased income for farmers and farm workers, and therefore contribute to poverty alleviation. For the commercial partner Lionheart this would mean more raw materi
nontech.tech_reason: The project enhances farmer productivity and income through technology adoption and training, directly targeting underserved agricultural communities for economic inclusion.

nontech.text: Let's green Kenya — The aim of the initiative is the development of Treedom agroforestry projects in Kenya through the strengthening of plant nurseries and the training and awareness of local communities. Treedom is a web platform that allows its users to plant a tree remotely and follow it online, offering small farmers all over the world direct social and economic benefits. The business model envisages direct financing of producer cooperatives to carry out the tree planting and maintenance service, following a specific code of conduct called Treedom Standard. The main expected results are: the enhancement of 3 nurseries with a capacity of 20,000 seedlings each for online sale, the training of n. 3 technicians in management/maintenance of the nursery, and of n. 260 producers on plants care and agroforestry systems, the improvement of the economic situation for 650 families, the improvem
nontech.tech_reason: The project trains local producers and technicians, builds community capacity, and uses a digital platform to connect users with tree planting, directly promoting digital literacy and inclusion.

nontech.text: Fighting hunger: smart agriculture techniques for home gardens in chin state Myanmar — The project is being implemented in some villages in Southern Chin which have the highest rates of poverty and malnutrition, in order to create a network of vegetable gardens managed by farming families and assisted by the project agronomists, together with the supply of seeds and cuttings of good quality. The project aims to improve the state of nutrition and reduce the poverty rate in the state of Chin, spreading sustainable agricultural practices and diversifying agricultural production. The planned activities aim to increase the knowledge and skills of the families of beneficiary farmers in the application of smart agriculture and organic farming practices, increase the knowledge and skills of the beneficiary families in the management of family gardens, increase in annual family income per benefic
nontech.tech_reason: The project builds digital skills and knowledge among farming families to adopt smart agriculture techniques, directly targeting underserved rural communities for inclusive capacity building.

nontech.text: Multidisciplinary technologies for the study and conservation of Post Byzantine monasteries in Southern Albania — The project, active since 2019, investigates the post-Byzantine monasteries of southern Albania, which constitute heritage of extraordinary value, currently under threat. The project seeks to support the study and safeguarding of three religious complexes, applying an integrated program that starts from historical-architectural research and develops with innovative 3D scanning and modelling technologies, georadar diagnostics, semantic HBIM platform for the conservation project. Courses are foreseen for local officials and experts.
nontech.tech_reason: The project trains local officials and experts in advanced digital conservation technologies, directly building digital skills and capacity within an underserved cultural heritage community.

nontech.text: Pilot Project, Hadrianopolis (Albania): archaeological survey and valorization of the archaeological park — The Project, active since 2005 and led by Prof. Roberto Perna, in collaboration with the Albanian Archaeological Institute and the Regional Directorate of Monuments of Gjirokaster (DRKK), involves implementing archaeological investigations, restoration, staff training and planning and management of the Archaeological Park of Hadrianopolis-Antigone and the Drino Valley, including through the use of innovative technologies, applying the Strategic Management Plan of the Drino Valley drafted in collaboration between the University of Macerata and DRKK.
nontech.tech_reason: The project uses digital technologies for archaeological survey and park management, aligning with digital governance through innovative tech application in cultural heritage management.

nontech.text: Rock painting in Cappadocia: a project of knowledge, conservation and valorization — The project has been active in Turkey since 1996. Since 2006 the project has been underway of knowledge and valorization of the pictorial heritage of the Nevsehir region and the project to restore the Tokali church in the Open Air Museum of Goreme. Extensive research of the territory has resulted in an online database that supports the understanding of the rocky habitat and the pictorial findings in relation to the rural landscape.
nontech.tech_reason: The project creates an online database and supports digital documentation and valorization of cultural heritage, aligning with digital public services and digital heritage management.

nontech.text: WELLNESS AGRICULTURE DEVELOPMENT WORK INCLUSION - WORK INCLUSION PROJECT IN THE AGRICULTURAL SECTOR FOR WOMEN OF KAJIADO COUNTY, KENYA. — The project proposal aims to contribute to the working inclusion of Kajiado women by supporting both their empowerment process and their well-being, through the rental of a field of about 8 hectares (20 acres) where to produce aromatic herbs thanks to the work of 12 greenhouses with drip irrigation system, and other agricultural products destined for the local and international market. The specific objectives set are to initiate women from the Maasai community in Kajiado county into agricultural work and guarantee them monthly health coverage. The expected results for each activity: 2 7500 people are sensitized offline and online about the activities carried out and the relations between Kenya and Italy. 40 Maasai women residing in Kajiado county get a
nontech.tech_reason: The project empowers Maasai women through agricultural training, job creation, and socio-economic inclusion, aligning with digital inclusion objectives of community empowerment and capacity building.

nontech.text: Re connection 2.0 - reconciliation between Kosovo and Serbia through cooperation in the field of culture. — The overall objective of the selected action  '' Reconnection 2.0'',  is to promote cooperation ,intercultural dialogue, people to people connections through culture.  in Kosovo and Serbia.The specific objective of the project is to use a long lasting partnership between two strong CSO actors in regional scene to launch a Reconnection 2.0 as a platform that will enable exchange and cooperation between Kosovo and Serbia in cultural sector, including theatre performances, literature translations, stage readings, debates, round tables.
nontech.tech_reason: The project fosters intercultural dialogue and people-to-people connections through cultural cooperation, targeting underserved communities and promoting inclusion via accessible cultural exchange platforms.

nontech.text: Développement d'un maraichage péri-urbain agroécologique encourageant le mutualisme entre les acteurs des territoires.Maraichage Agroécologique Peri-urbain (MARIGO) — Le projet MARIGO concerne la transition agroécologique des pratiques maraichères en zones péri urbaines qui contribueront à une transformation durable et productive de l'agriculture et des systèmes alimentaires de la Côte d'Ivoire.Ce projet de recherche/action propose de développer des interactions à bénéfices réciproques (mutualisme) entre les acteurs des territoires peri-urbains et en particulier entre maraichers et éleveurs mais aussi entre producteurs et consommateurs, favorisant une économie circulaire locale et des circuits courts d'approvisionnement. Il propose de constituer une plateforme constituée d'agriculteurs, de formateurs, de chercheurs, d'acteurs de la filière maraichère, de décideurs de politiques publiques
nontech.tech_reason: The project involves building a collaborative platform and training ecosystem for peri-urban farmers, which aligns with digital inclusion through capacity building and access to digital tools for underserved agricultural communities.

nontech.text: Strengthened the health response in the rural area of Dianra Village and Marandallah (Béré-Ivory Coast Region) — &#34,El proyecto responde a la necesidad de mejorar la calidad de la atención sanitaria en la zona rural de Dianra Village y Marandallah (Región Béré-Costa de Marfil) tras el impacto del covid-19 en el sistema de salud de la región.Para ello se trabajarán tres líneas durante un año de ejecución:- La primera fortalecerá la salud comunitaria y de proximidad de estas áreas, ya que la distancia entre la población y la oferta sanitaria es muy grande. Es fundamental garantizar una proximidad real con la gente, lo que implica una programación mensual de visitas a los pueblos de nuestras amplias zonas sanitarias para prevenir, sensibilizar, informar, vacunar, proponer los primeros cuidados.- Con la segunda línea del proyecto se pretende mejorar la cobertura que brindan los dos centros
nontech.tech_reason: The project expands health services through telecardiology and digital mental health support, directly improving digital access and inclusion for rural communities.

Subsequently, lessons and training units are to take place on chemical-free cultivation areas, as well as online. An evaluation will take place in the last weeks of the project. Through teaching sessions on the prepared fields, the farmers will be taught the knowledge and skills for soil remediation and sustainable agriculture. nontech.text: TBL Defending the role of civil society — IMPACT 23-0000357 - Defending the role of civil society Space for civil society is shrinking. When this proposal was submitted, GYLA was concerned with a growing hostility against CS actors, coming from the authorities and turning the public against them. However, at the moment Georgian Parliament is considering a law on foreign agents aimed to stigmatise the whole civil society. GYLA is in a lead on several platforms coming up against this law but also needs financial resources to systematise its work and reach out to the public with explanations of the advantages of a free SC. Within the project, GYLA will formalise a CSO Network (about10 national and 10 local NGOs) that will - hold meetings in Tbilisi and in regions, - litigate 4 strategic cases , - conduct 20 legal consultations , - and organise a vigorous awareness raising campaign online wi
nontech.tech_reason: The project focuses on building civil society capacity, legal advocacy, and public awareness through digital storytelling and online campaigns to support inclusion and protect civil society space.

nontech.text: Advisor for maintenance and repair of facilities of Mukuru Promotion Centre, Nairobi — The organisation started as a temporary school in the Mukuru slums and its key objective was to provide education to the children of Mukuru to get them off the streets. Mukuru Promotion Centre runs four primary schools, a secondary school and an   adult education centre. On vocational training, the MPC runs a vocational centre offering courses in computer, beauty and hairdressing, dress making, plumbing and masonry, catering as well as art. Since 1st March 2016, a technical advisor was   assigned to support selected MPC staff in repair and maintenance works for MPC schools and institutions. When the workload is heavier, selected jobs are given to local tradesmen who carry it out under the supervision of the TA.
nontech.tech_reason: The project supports vocational training and capacity building for underserved communities, aligning with digital inclusion goals.

nontech.text: Solar driven agri-food processes — Uganda face serious issues in the Agri-Food sector. It contributes significantly to GDP and is the main source of employment. However, a substantial proportion of the harvest spoils and destabilizes farmer's income. There is a lack of knowledge  and access to effective post-harvest handling methods and impacts on the living conditions of the rural population. Thus, there is an urgent need to provide affordable, renewable energy and knowhow for food conservation and food processing.   solFOOD combines the knowledge of solar energy technology with state-of-the-art food processing technology. The approach determines a matrix of food conservation applications showing the socio-economic-technical impact of solar energy  systems. This includes hybrid solutions based on solar thermal energy and photovoltaic. The project also addresses specific needs in advance
nontech.tech_reason: The project centers on building a solar-powered agri-food processing plant, which is physical infrastructure for renewable energy and food processing technology.

nontech.text: Let's green Kenya — The aim of the initiative is the development of Treedom agroforestry projects in Kenya through the strengthening of plant nurseries and the training and awareness of local communities. Treedom is a web platform that allows its users to plant a tree remotely and follow it online, offering small farmers all over the world direct social and economic benefits. The business model envisages direct financing of producer cooperatives to carry out the tree planting and maintenance service, following a specific code of conduct called Treedom Standard. The main expected results are: the enhancement of 3 nurseries with a capacity of 20,000 seedlings each for online sale, the training of n. 3 technicians in management/maintenance of the nursery, and of n. 260 producers on plants care and agroforestry systems, the improvement of the economic situation for 650 families, the improvem
nontech.tech_reason: The project trains local producers and technicians, builds community capacity, and uses a digital platform to connect users with tree planting, directly promoting digital skills and inclusion.

nontech.text: Project Durres: Archaeological Research and Valorization of the Archaeological Heritage of Durres and the Surrounding Territory — The archaeological work has been underway since 2004, led by Prof. Sonia Antonelli and involves archaeological research of the amphitheatre and geological-archaeological and archaeological-seismological surveys of the city of Durrës and the surrounding territory. nontech.tech_reason: The project emphasizes training students and local participants in advanced archaeological technologies, promoting digital skills and capacity building within an underserved academic and cultural context.

nontech.text: Rock painting in Cappadocia: a project of knowledge, conservation and valorization — The project has been active in Turkey since 1996. Since 2006 the project has been underway of knowledge and valorization of the pictorial heritage of the Nevsehir region and the project to restore the Tokali church in the Open Air Museum of Goreme. Extensive research of the territory has resulted in an online database that supports the understanding of the rocky habitat and the pictorial findings in relation to the rural landscape. The mission mitigates CO2 emissions or applies green technologies or behaviors during the research.

nontech.text: Archaeological Investigation and Conservation Restoration of the Cham Monuments of My Son — The mission pursues the following objectives: cataloguing, inventory and risk assessment of the Cham monuments in My Son and Quang Nam Province with the new version of the SMDB System created by the Lerici Foundation for these purposes and in particular for sites in Asia, excavation of Group L and its consolidation, training of restorers in the Restoration Laboratory at the Museum of Quang Nam Province in Tam Ky.

nontech.text: Ukrenergo Transmission Network Emergency Restoration - Gender and Inclusion TC support — The EBRD has approved a financing package that will deliver 372 million EUR to support Ukraine's electricity transmission company, Ukrenergo. The money will be used for swift emergency repairs following heavy bombings of civilian power infrastructure during Russia's war on Ukraine. The Project is strategically important since it will enable the Company to secure energy supply to population and businesses throughout the country and ensure the stability of the country's energy system. The Project will also strengthen the Ukrainian power system's ability to maintain commercial flows with ENTSO-E (European Network of Transmission System Operators for Electricity). The linked technical assistance financed by the CEI Fund at the EBRD will provide support concerning Ukrenergo's recent integration into ENTSO
nontech.purpose: Electric power transmission and distribution (centralised grids)
nontech.tech_reason: The project includes gender-focused training and inclusion initiatives for managers and employees, aligning with digital inclusion objectives.

nontech.text: Good Drinking Water for City of Rivne — The aim of the project is to support the quality of life and health of Rivne-city residents through the sharing of experience in the modernization of water management systems and the use of modern technologies in water management, through the sustainable development of outdated infrastructure. Therefore, the project will aim to achieve the following results: 1. increase awareness of the professional public about ensuring the safe supply of drinking water through the installation of smaller technological equipment and on the operation of these facilities in water companies in the Slovak Republic, 2. in cooperation with representatives of partner organizations, prepare a proposal of a specific technical and technological solution for self-production of sodium hypochlorite for disinfection of drinking water at a specific water treatment plant Kyivska
nontech.tech_reason: The project involves designing and deploying a technical solution for water treatment and includes digital documentation, training, and operational support, aligning with e‑government‑style ICT interventions.

nontech.text: Football for Gender Equality – improving access to sport for girls — The project aims to renovate and equip a women's locker room in the club premises, which will serve about 100 female players. It will also create office space for the women's football team with modern computer and projection technology. The creation of spaces for sporting activities for girls and women from the town and the surrounding area will have a positive impact on health, social inclusion and gender equality. This is particularly important in an economically less developed and highly patriarchal part of the country.
nontech.tech_reason: The project enhances access to digital tools and spaces for girls and women, supporting digital inclusion through technology-enabled facilities.

nontech.text: The main objective of the CLIMATHON ALBANIA project is to improve the quality of life and health of the people of Albania by introducing innovations to improve sustainability and environmental protection. The project is focused on the transfer of practical experience from the successful implementation of innovation events CLIMATHON BRATISLAVA. The event involves startups, technology companies, student teams, and experienced mentors whose goal is to create prototypes of innovative solutions in 48 hours that respond to the challenges the cities are facing. CLIMATHON ALBANIA will be attended by more than 170 participants who will develop at least 15 innovative solutions. nontech.tech_reason: The project emphasizes capacity building, gender inclusion, and empowering diverse participants (including women and youth) to develop climate solutions, aligning with digital inclusion goals.

nontech.text: Permaculture Action for Sustainable Savings And Greening Entrepreneurship for school children (PASSAGE) — The PASSAGE project aims to promote financial self-reliance of vulnerable school children and their households in Amuru district, Acholi, Uganda.10 state schools are involved in the educational project. In addition, permaculture clubs will be formed in 20 villages in the region. Participants of school and village clubs are trained in entrepreneurship education as well as to participate in dialogue in their communities and e.g. radio talk shows to promote human rights. Those involved in the project are also trained and supported in economics (saving), adopting good nutrition and protecting the environment.About 200,000 people live in Amuru district. More than half of them are under 20 years of age. The area is very poor. The Acholi region has the lowest proportion of food-poor househo
nontech.tech_reason: The project trains school children, mothers, and teachers in financial literacy, entrepreneurship, and permaculture, directly building digital and economic skills for underserved communities.

nontech.text: Introducing children safety education project 'Protect yourself and help others' in Georgia — The main goal of the cooperation project is to introduce children safety program 'Protect yourself and help others in Georgia, to share Estonia's experience with the relevant state structures and teachers of Kobuleti Municipality.Estonian experts are going to visit Georgian partners to share the method and program of the study and Georgian partners are invited to Estonia to observe safety education in Estonian schools and safety camps. Description of the study method and content material will be translated to Georgian and given forward digitally.
nontech.tech_reason: The project focuses on teaching children safety through digital education and capacity‑building, targeting underserved youth and promoting digital skills and inclusion.

nontech.text: Act for the Environment! Keep it clean cooperation program in Georgia — The aim of the project is to prevent illegal dumping in the Khashur and Kaspi regions of Georgia, in cooperation between communities and local governments. As a result of the project, the awareness about the impact of waste on nature has increased significantly, a new social norm has been created that illegal waste dumping is not acceptable. The waste bins and cigarette butt boxes have been placed in the so-called waste hot spots of the regions and they will be kept clean and waste-free also after finishing the project. Technology will be used for mapping waste.The end result of the project a successful, sustainable form of cooperation to address the problem of illegal dumping and prevent uncontrolled littering has created, which can be implemented by other regions of Georgia.
nontech.tech_reason: The project uses technology for waste mapping and community engagement to promote digital literacy and inclusive environmental stewardship.

nontech.text: Repair and arrangement of a bomb shelter in the village of Chervona Dolyna, Mykolaiv region — The village of Chervona Dolyna, which is part of the Shuroke village community in Mykolaiv region, was right on the front line for eight months. the villages have been subjected to numerous enemy attacks.Education in the community is conducted online, because being on the front line did not allow them to set up shelters. Moreover, the children of Chervona Dolyna have no place to study, as their local lyceum has been reduced to rubble.
nontech.tech_reason: The project provides essential digital access for displaced children to continue online education, directly supporting digital inclusion for an underserved group.

nontech.text: Bringing Upmade know-how to Kenya — The project aims to reduce textile waste and greenhouse gas emissions in Kenya's garment industry through recycling. EKA, SEI and Moi University are collaborating with the Rivatex factory to introduce the UPMADE concept. Rivatex offers on-site assistance to establish direct links with these industrial companies. UPMADE provides the manufacturer with specific know-how through an industrial recycling process that is in line with the zero-waste design of the circular economy.
nontech.tech_reason: The project supports textile waste reduction and circular economy practices in Kenya's garment industry, which can be interpreted as promoting sustainable digital inclusion through technology-enabled recycling processes.

nontech.text: Strengthened the health response in the rural area of Dianra Village and Marandallah (Béré-Ivory Coast Region) — &#34,El proyecto responde a la necesidad de mejorar la calidad de la atención sanitaria en la zona rural de Dianra Village y Marandallah (Región Béré-Costa de Marfil) tras el impacto del covid-19 en el sistema de salud de la región.Para ello se trabajarán tres líneas durante un año de ejecución:- La primera fortalecerá la salud comunitaria y de proximidad de estas áreas, ya que la distancia entre la población y la oferta sanitaria es muy grande. Es fundamental garantizar una proximidad real con la gente, lo que implica una programación mensual de visitas a los pueblos de nuestras amplias zonas sanitarias para prevenir, sensibilizar, informar, vacunar, proponer los primeros cuidados.- Con la segunda línea del proyecto se pretende mejorar la cobertura que brindan los dos centros
nontech.tech_reason: The project expands health services through telecardiology and digital mental health support, directly improving digital access and inclusion for rural communities.

nontech.text: Land-mines and unexploded ordnance safety awareness in Kharkiv area — The main objective of the project is to increase awareness of population of Kharkiv region on understanding mine and other unexploded ordnance safety. It aims at strengthening the resilience of the population in the Kharkiv region, and Ukraine as a whole, against the risks associated with residual mines and unexploded ordnance. This comprises knowledge, experience, training scenarios, but also education, which will enable better cooperation with local authorities. The overall goal is to help the population return to a normal way of life as quickly as possible. A necessary condition for this return is the minimization of hardship and the impacts of war. nontech.text: EU Support to the Agency for Medicines and Medical Devices of Bosnia and Herzegovina — Due to the late adoption of the budget for many years, the Agency for Medicines and Medical Devices of BiH was not able to renew the IT equipment necessary for adequate monitoring of   the procedures for registration of medicines and medical devices. The majority of trafficked persons in Nigeria are from southern and south eastern states. Edo State is the epicenter of the epidemic, which is increasingly spreading to its neighbors, such as Delta and Anambra States. Trafficking has the most profound detrimental effects on human rights and increases state fragility by undermining both governmental authority and capacity. As a result, preventing and combatting trafficking is a crucial area for intervention from a human security perspective and a key topic on the agenda of the Swiss-Nigerian Migration Partnership. nontech.tech_reason: The project uses social media communication channels to raise awareness about trafficking risks, targeting underserved communities and expanding digital outreach to prevent human trafficking, aligning with digital inclusion objectives.

nontech.text: Myanmar Flexible Funding 2024 — Flexible funding til Myanmar. The intervention will support a total of 8,850 beneficiaries through education and child protection services. In Education, SC will support the children aged 5-11 through the community-based education activities through Open Learning Materials, Social Emotional Learning resources and through the capacity enhancement of educators. In Child Protection, SC will support girls and boys under 18 who are at risk of or experiencing various kinds of abuse - violence, neglect and exploitation.
nontech.tech_reason: The project supports education and child protection for vulnerable children, focusing on capacity building and access to services for underserved groups, aligning with digital inclusion objectives.

nontech.text: Integrated Drought Management System and measures to mitigate the impact of the climate change in Kosovo — The main objective is to Support sustainable development of the water management and economic growth in Kosovo as result of the climate change through the measures for integrated drought management. The following specific objectives of the project are: 1. To improve the drought management of the water resources as consequence of the climate change via application of the mitigation measures, 2. To increase technical and personal capacities on drought management and public awareness about drought impacts on the water resources through training, workshops and dialogues on mitigation measures. The outcomes will be: 1.1. Proposal of the Drought Action Plan for Drin River Basin as integrated part of the River Basin Management Plan as required by the EU Water Framework Directive. 1.2. Port
nontech.tech_reason: The project involves creating a drought management platform and digital consultation processes, which are digital governance tools for public service delivery and stakeholder engagement.

nontech.text: The main objective of the CLIMATHON ALBANIA project is to improve the quality of life and health of the people of Albania by introducing innovations to improve sustainability and environmental protection. The project is focused on the transfer of practical experience from the successful implementation of innovation events CLIMATHON BRATISLAVA. The event involves startups, technology companies, student teams, and experienced mentors whose goal is to create prototypes of innovative solutions in 48 hours that respond to the challenges the cities are facing. CLIMATHON ALBANIA will be attended by more than 170 participants who will develop at least 15 innovative solutions. The project has two specific objectives, (1) to raise awareness of green innovation and capacity building in cities to implement green and innovative solutions, and (2) to implement innovative solutions that will contribute
nontech.tech_reason: The project emphasizes capacity building, gender inclusion, and empowering diverse participants (including women and youth) to develop climate solutions, aligning with digital inclusion goals.

nontech.text: Budget Support for the development of the Uzbekistan¿s agri-food sector and resilient rural livelihoods — The overall objective of this action is ¿to contribute to the development of a sustainable market oriented and inclusive agri-food system that will increase farm income, expand rural livelihood opportunities and enhance food security for smallholder farmers, women and the most vulnerable groups in Uzbekistan¿. The specific objectives are to: 1) Support the implementation framework of the agri-food development strategy, enabling inclusive development of a climate smart agri-food system  and 2) Support sustainable livelihood opportunities, decent jobs and access to markets for the most vulnerable (smallholders, MSMEs, youth, women, disabled, migrants). The action shall be instrumental in sustaining the pace of reforms defined in the Uzbekistan Agri-food Development Strategy 2020-2030,
nontech.tech_reason: The project emphasizes budget support for reforms in public finance management, budget transparency, oversight, and strategic planning, which aligns with digital governance of government systems and reforms.

nontech.text: Re connection 2.0 - reconciliation between Kosovo and Serbia through cooperation in the field of culture. — The overall objective of the selected action  '' Reconnection 2.0'',  is to promote cooperation ,intercultural dialogue, people to people connections through culture.  in Kosovo and Serbia. nontech.text: Développement d¿un maraichage péri-urbain agroécologique encourageant le mutualisme entre les acteurs des territoires.  Maraichage Agroécologique Peri — Le projet MARIGO concerne la transition agroécologique des pratiques maraichères en zones péri urbaines qui contribueront à une transformation durable et productive de l¿agriculture et des systèmes alimentaires de la Côte d¿Ivoire.    Ce projet de recherche/action propose de développer des interactions à bénéfices réciproques (mutualisme) entre les acteurs des territoires peri-urbains et en particulier entre maraichers et éleveurs mais aussi entre producteurs et consommateurs, favorisant une économie circulaire locale et des circuits courts d¿approvisionnement. Il propose de constituer une plateforme constituée d¿agriculteurs, de formateurs, de chercheurs, d¿acteurs de la filière maraichère, de décideurs de politiques publiques sectoriel
nontech.tech_reason: The project builds a collaborative platform and involves capacity building among diverse stakeholders to improve digital access and participation in agriculture.

nontech.text: F.a: Building Union Power in the Metal and Mining Unions in Indonesia — The project will organise and build capacities of Indonesian metal and mining union leaders and members, with a special attention on women and youth to institutionalise their participation in unions' leadership and activities. Capacity building will focus on the ability to campaign for union representation and occupational healthcare system committees at workplaces, ratification of the ILO conventions 176, 183 and 190, and for amendment of the so-called Omnibus law in Indonesia. Organising, campaigning and collective negotiations will explore the use of technology to facilitate and improve unions' work. The project will also level-up the dialogue between educational / training institutions and trade unions to integrate trade union education in the academic and vocational curriculum, particularly for the graduating se
nontech.tech_reason: The project uses technology to strengthen union organization and participation, especially among women and youth, aligning with digital inclusion goals.

nontech.text: Permaculture Action for Sustainable Savings And Greening Entrepreneurship for school children (PASSAGE) — The PASSAGE project aims to promote financial self-reliance of vulnerable school children and their households in Amuru district, Acholi, Uganda.10 state schools are involved in the educational project. In addition, permaculture clubs will be formed in 20 villages in the region. Participants of school and village clubs are trained in entrepreneurship education as well as to participate in dialogue in their communities and e.g. radio talk shows to promote human rights. Those involved in the project are also trained and supported in economics (saving), adopting good nutrition and protecting the environment.About 200,000 people live in Amuru district. More than half of them are under 20 years of age. The area is very poor. The Acholi region has the lowest proportion of food-poor househo
nontech.tech_reason: The project trains school children and community members in financial literacy, entrepreneurship, and uses radio and digital platforms to promote rights and market products, directly enhancing digital skills and inclusion.

nontech.text: WaterNet:Safe drinking water in Ukraine — NGO WaterNet propose the project Safe drinking water in Ukraine: Quality assessment and methods of control for 20 months duration aims to raise awareness about the drinking water quality in Ukraine during the war and sharing the knowledges about modern water treatment methods and technologies. nontech.text: Assistance in Education — Strengthening the institutional capacity of the Department of French Language and Literature within the Faculty of Philosophy of the University of Sarajevo, Bosnia and Herzegovina. The implementation of this project aimed to strengthen the institutional capacity of the Department of French Language and Literature within the Faculty of Philosophy at the University of Sarajevo, Bosnia and Herzegovina. RoAid provided a donation of 62,500 lei (equivalent to 12,500 EUR) to the Department of French Language and Literature at the Faculty of Philosophy, intended for the modernization of classroom and office equipment. The donation facilitated the purchase of laptops, smartboards, projectors, chairs, and printers, as well as improvements to overall classroom conditions through the installation of air conditioning units for heating and cooling and the painting/renovation
nontech.tech_reason: The project upgrades classroom and office equipment and improves learning environments, directly enhancing digital access and inclusion for students and staff.
