nontech.text: CapaViet3 - Remediation of contaminated sites in Bac Ninh province — The aim of CapaViet 3 is to inform about (technical) options for action to remediate contaminated land. Through a combination of technology transfer by co-creating problem-solving strategies with local partners and training activities such as an online course, fact sheets and an exhibition, the project strengthens the capacity of authorities, educational institutions and decision-makers in the management of contaminated sites in Viet Nam.
nontech.tech_reason: The project builds institutional capacity and digital tools for managing contaminated sites, aligning with e-government and digital public service objectives.

This goal will be achieved through the following measures: 1) Technical enhancements to the existing IT platform for discussing environmental issues and developing international partnerships (hereinafter: IT platform), 2) Organization of a one-week eco-educational tour for a Kazakh industry group (consisting of representatives from politics and business) with a continuing edu
nontech.tech_reason: The project focuses on knowledge transfer and capacity building for waste management governance using an IT platform and policy-oriented collaboration, aligning with digital governance objectives.

nontech.text: Special Assistance Program in Ukraine — ContextCommunities near the frontlines are particularly affected by Russia's war of aggression. Many areas have been severely damaged or temporarily occupied, forcing millions to flee. Almost 6.5 million people have left Ukraine, while 3.4 million have relocated to safe regions within the country.Even before the war, rural areas struggled to provide healthcare and social services due to outdated facilities, limited resources, and insufficient training opportunities. The war has worsened these issues, destroying infrastructure, displacing trained personnel, and disrupting energy supplies. Meanwhile, demand for services has soared as vulnerable groups - internally displaced persons, injured individuals, veterans, and jobseekers - require urgent medical and social support.ObjectiveGovernmental and non-governmental institutions in selected Ukrainian co
nontech.tech_reason: The project enhances government service delivery through digital equipment, IT systems, and modular repairs to restore administrative and health services in war-affected areas.

nontech.text: Conservation Temple Area Phung Thien in Hué, Vietnam, 2023-2025 — Continued conservation/restoration of the preserved building stock of the area of Phung Tien Temple in the imperial city of Hué, Vietnam (UNESCO cultural heritate site) with integrated courses and workshops on preservation of cultural heritage for university and school students. For the first time, a concept of virtual reconstruction of lost architecture is developed that could serve as an example for other cultural heritage sites in Vietnam.
nontech.tech_reason: The project involves digital reconstruction and virtual modeling of cultural heritage, representing a digital innovation in heritage management and public service delivery.

nontech.text: Improving energy efficiency in the building sector through the use of renewable raw materials from agroforestry systems — More than half of primary energy consumption in Uzbekistan is being spent on the energy supply for the building sector. There is great need to increase efficiency through improved insulation of houses and to substitute carbon-intensive building materials with locally available bio-based resources. Based on locally adapted building design, materials, and improved production methods, the project develops resource-saving alternatives that combine both traditional and modern construction using wood, agricultural residues and clay. To demonstrate the advantages of such building systems it will build a model house in Tashkent. Educational materials will be developed that focus on the systemic relationships and synergetic benefits between agroforestry, ecosystem services, ho
nontech.tech_reason: The project delivers digital learning materials and e-learning content to expand knowledge on sustainable building practices across Central Asia, targeting underserved educational groups.

nontech.text: Integrated and innovative maritime technologies for mobility and fisheries — The Marshall Islands are a small low-lying atoll nation in the Pacific Ocean. Its very existence depends on ambitious global emission reduction to limit sea level rise. Despite this, it heavily relies on costly imported fossil fuels for its vital sea transport and fishing sector. The country works towards developing a zero-emission fleet as part of ongoing initiatives, but a successful paradigm shift throughout society is still pending. The project merges emission-free boat design and construction, indigenous knowledge and modern maritime training for small-scale transport and fisheries. This breaks social and gender barriers, combining efforts in society and the private sector. Integrating innovative technologies into formal education fosters societal sustainability and resilience. The project sends out a stron
nontech.tech_reason: The project emphasizes digital literacy, indigenous knowledge integration, and community empowerment through innovative maritime technologies, directly targeting underserved groups and societal inclusion.

Its very existence depends on ambitious global emission reduction to limit sea level rise. Despite this, it heavily relies on costly imported fossil fuels for its vital sea transport and fishing sector. The country works towards developing a zero-emission fleet as part of ongoing initiatives, but a successful paradigm shift throughout society is still pending. The project merges emission-free boat design and construction, indigenous knowledge and modern maritime training for small-scale transport and fisheries. This breaks social and gender barriers, combining efforts in society and the private sector. Integrating innovative technologies into formal education fosters societal sustainability and resilience. The project sends out a stron
nontech.tech_reason: The project emphasizes capacity building, gender equity, and community empowerment through innovative maritime technologies, directly aligning with digital inclusion objectives.

nontech.text: Vocational Education and Training in Ukraine \ Skills4Recovery — Context:Communities near the frontlines are particularly affected by Russia's war of aggression. Many areas have been severely damaged or temporarily occupied, forcing millions to flee. Almost 6.5 million people have left Ukraine, while 3.4 million have relocated to safe regions within the country.Even before the war, rural areas struggled to provide healthcare and social services due to outdated facilities, limited resources, and insufficient training opportunities. The war has worsened these issues, destroying infrastructure, displacing trained personnel, and disrupting energy supplies. Meanwhile, demand for services has soared as vulnerable groups - internally displaced persons, injured individuals, veterans, and jobseekers - require urgent medical and social support.Objective:Governmental and non-governmental institutio
nontech.tech_reason: The project enhances government capacity to deliver essential services through digital equipment, IT systems, and digital service provision in war-affected communities.

nontech.text: Vocational Education and Training in Georgia's Growth Sectors — ContextThe shortage of skilled workers in Georgia is limiting economic growth and new employment opportunities. As impractical vocational education and training (VET) programmes continue to fall short, the demand for skilled labour remains unmet. Moreover, micro, small, and medium-sized enterprises (MSMEs) struggle with limited expertise, while business associations fail to provide the necessary services, and advocacy needed to meet the demands of the private industry. Consequently, Georgia must act to modernise its VET system and enhance its workforce to remain competitive.ObjectiveEmployment opportunities in selected industries of the Georgian economy are enhanced, offering improved prospects for growth and development.ApproachThe project, in collaboration with the development organisation SEQUA as a service provider, aims
nontech.tech_reason: The project builds digital skills and capacity for vocational training, MSMEs, and private industry associations to improve workforce inclusion and competitiveness.

nontech.tech_reason: The project involves advising on laws and regulations, promoting models for managing forests, and integrating sustainable supply chains, which aligns with digital governance aspects such as policy reform and digital public services.

nontech.text: 20-PBSP - More with Less in the Philippine Coconut Industry — 'More with Less in the Philippine Coconut Industry'The 'More with Less in the Philippine Coconut Industry' project by PBSP, Lionheart and PCA will contribute to SDG8 by stimulating higher levels of economic activity in the coconut sector through technological upgrading and innovation, and by focusing on safe and labor intensive production of high-value added products from coconut sap. By introducing planting materials of superior variety, coupled with environmental friendly Good Agricultural Practices, yield will increase. Introducing innovative technologies in sap tapping will make the task less difficult, thereby increasing productivity and generating jobs. These twin interventions result in increased income for farmers and farm workers, and therefore contribute to poverty alleviation. For the commercial partner Lionheart th
nontech.tech_reason: The project enhances farmer productivity and income through technology adoption and training, directly supporting digital inclusion by improving livelihoods and access to innovative agricultural practices.

nontech.text: Humanitarian aid to the refugee population of Bidibidi for access to health, through the provision of solar energy equipment in the Health Center — Título completo del proyecto: Ayuda humanitaria a la población refugiada de Bidibidi para el acceso a la salud, mediante la dotación de equipo de energía solar en el Centro de Salud KORO Level III HEALTH CENTER en el campo de refugiados de Bidibidi, Uganda :: Esta intervención se dirige a ofrecer ayuda humanitaria a la población del campo de refugiados de Bidibidi, el asentamiento de refugiados más grande de Uganda y el segundo más poblado de África.En concreto la intervención propone la dotación de energía solar en uno de los quince centros de salud de los que dispone el Campo, el de la zona 4, El Centro de Salud Koro Level III HEALTH CENTRE. Se encuentra en un lugar de difícil acceso fuera de la red eléctrica nacional. La instalación de est
nontech.tech_reason: The solar equipment enables internet-connected computer equipment in a health center, directly supporting digital access and literacy for underserved refugee communities.

nontech.text: Promotion of the right to equitable and inclusive education for children and adolescents in vulnerability, through the improvement of infrastructure — El proyecto promueve el Derecho a la Educación en 20 escuelas de Mfangano, priorizando a niñas adolescentes. Mejora infraestructuras con enfoque de género, aborda higiene menstrual, previene violencia sexual mediante formación y refuerza el acceso a la justicia. También mejora la calidad educativa con aulas, bibliotecas y formación digital según la nueva reforma. Kenya. AOD: no - Localización: 20 COMUNIDADES, ISLA DE MFANGANO, HOMA BAY
nontech.tech_reason: The project enhances educational access and digital literacy for vulnerable girls and adolescents through digital training and infrastructure, directly supporting digital inclusion.

nontech.text: Better Work Phase IV — Improving working conditions and productivity in the global garment sector: Promoting compliance with national law and international core labour standards and supporting factories benefits workers, managers, countries and consumers and delivers a better normal in recovering from the Covid-19 pandemic. The global garment sector is going through a period of profound and rapid change. It faces significant challenges to ensure a human-centred approach to technological advances, digitalization and automation. Demographic shifts are changing where apparel is pro-duced and sold. Extreme heat, air pollution, flooding, reduced availability of water, and the generation of chemical and other waste exacerbate occupational safety and health risks undermine productivity. These challenges were clearly exposed and exacerbated by the Covid-19 pandemic. Better Work will contribute t
nontech.tech_reason: The project promotes compliance with national labor laws and international standards through digital monitoring and reporting systems, enhancing governance of labor practices in the garment sector.

nontech.text: Aide à l'autonomie à Bali — Aide à l'autonomie pour les habitants de la région de Muntigunung à Bali : ateliers et sensibilisation à divers thèmes tels que la santé, l'hygiène, la grossesse, l'alimentation, l'amélioration des conditions de vie, l'apiculture, la formation Mise en place d'un approvisionnement durable en eau : construction de toits servant à la collecte de l'eau. Cette eau est ensuite stockée dans des réservoirs et alimente les habitants même pendant la saison sèche.
nontech.tech_reason: The project enhances digital literacy and community empowerment through workshops and training on health, hygiene, and sustainable practices, directly supporting underserved populations.

nontech.text: Improving the quality of healthcare services for Dnipro residents by preventing the transmission of germs and infections. — Joint efforts in the healthcare sector can have a major social impact. The project aims to improve the sanitary-epidemiological condition of the Municipal Clinical Hospital No. 16 and increase the quality of healthcare services provided to the residents of the partner municipality. In order to improve health care, it is important to use newer technologies. It was, therefore, decided to implement the project, which includes the purchase of a washing and disinfection machine and an exchange of experience/expertise between medical experts from both cities. The project will contribute to more efficient sterilization, overcoming the spread of antibiotic resistance and infections, maximizing patient safety by reducing the number of infections, reducing the turnaround time
nontech.tech_reason: The project centers on acquiring a washing and disinfection machine and enhancing sanitation processes, which are physical infrastructure components for infection control.

nontech.text: Improving drinking water supply and hygiene practices to strengthen the overall health status of rural households in Vietnam. — In four provinces in South Vietnam, poverty-stricken households have year-round access to safe drinking water and have introduced hygiene measures into their daily family lives that have led to improved health. To this end, water treatment plants are installed in 8 communities, managed and maintained by a trained water committee.Similarly, training in WASH is held in each community for 100 children and 100 adults. Participants pass on this knowledge to others in their community. Information boards are posted at water distribution points. An online platform provides further information and educational materials on the topic of safe water.
nontech.tech_reason: The project includes training, community education, and an online platform to disseminate hygiene knowledge, directly targeting digital literacy and inclusion for underserved rural populations.

nontech.text: Improving the training situation of young adults in Kenya by establishing a vocational training centre — The education and vocational training of young people in the rural district of Kisumu is to be promoted in order to provide them with better future opportunities through the acquisition of skills that can be used in the labour market. By the end of the project, 30 students are expected to have successfully completed one of the five one-year training courses (general agriculture, animal breeding, information technology/IT, beauty therapy/hairstyling and tailoring). Newly created, sustainable structures ensure that continuing education can be carried out on a permanent basis.
nontech.tech_reason: The project builds a vocational training centre to provide digital skills and IT training to young adults, directly targeting digital inclusion for underserved rural youth.
